steps.title: Identify the right angle using the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Check which combination of sides satisfies $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$:

$$440^2 + 384^2 = 193600 + 147456 = 341056 = 584^2$$

The right angle is at $S$, making $TR = 584$ the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Find tan(T) in the right triangle
steps.content:
In right triangle $RST$ with the right angle at $S$:
  • The side opposite angle $T$ is $RS = 440$
  • The side adjacent to angle $T$ is $ST = 384$
Apply the tangent definition to find:
$$\tan T = \frac{440}{384}$$

steps.title: Apply similarity and simplify
steps.content:
Since $T$ corresponds to $W$ in the similar triangles, $\angle T = \angle W$, so $\tan W = \tan T$. Simplify by dividing both numerator and denominator by their GCD, which is 8:

$$\tan W = \frac{440}{384} = \frac{55}{48}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
